title: RS11 Benchmark Results Summary
paper: LLMs and RAG for Medical Vocabulary Validation: A Systematic Review and Empirical Benchmark
hardware: Apple M1 MacBook Air, 8GB RAM, macOS Darwin 25.3.0
key_findings: Baseline LLM (no RAG): 42.9% accuracy — confirms LLMs cannot reliably look up exact terminology codes
ChromaDB RAG: 78.6% (+35.7pp) — vector retrieval dramatically improves code lookup
Abbreviation dictionary: 92.9-100% on focused 14-case set — domain knowledge bridges the last gap
Extended 42-case set: 71.4% (Config M) — reveals limitations with complex disambiguation and cross-mapping
Cross-encoder reranking HURT: -7.1pp — lexical/rule-based approaches outperform semantic re-ranking for exact code matching
100% reproducibility confirmed across 2 independent runs on 14-case set
reproducibility: Run 1 and Run 2 both produced identical results (14/14 = 100%) with Config J
